title: Make a non-deterministic action reproducible
description: A procedure to remove sources of non-determinism so an action produces identical outputs from identical inputs.
body: Use when a target's output differs between builds despite unchanged inputs. Do not rerun until it happens to match. Steps: diff the outputs of two builds to locate the varying bytes; trace them to timestamps, nondeterministic ordering, embedded paths, or parallelism; fix by sorting, stripping timestamps, and using stable paths. Validation signals include byte-identical outputs across repeated and cross-machine builds. Risk boundary: non-determinism breaks caching and reproducibility, so eliminate the variance at its source and verify with repeated builds.
